title: Most Valuable Customers by City and Plan
prompt: Identify the top 3 most valuable customers (by total fare paid) in each city and plan combination. For each of these customers, show their user_id, city, plan, and total fare paid. Order the results by city, plan, and total fare paid descending.
reference_sql:
WITH customer_fares AS (
    SELECT
        u.user_id,
        u.city,
        u.plan,
        SUM(t.fare_usd) AS total_fare
    FROM users u
    JOIN trips t ON u.user_id = t.user_id
    WHERE t.status = 'completed'
    GROUP BY u.user_id, u.city, u.plan
), ranked_customers AS (
    SELECT
        user_id,
        city,
        plan,
        total_fare,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY city, plan ORDER BY total_fare DESC) AS rank
    FROM customer_fares
)
SELECT
    user_id,
    city,
    plan,
    total_fare
FROM ranked_customers
WHERE rank <= 3
ORDER BY city, plan, total_fare DESC;
hint_notes: Key idea is to aggregate fare by user, city and plan; use window function to rank within city-plan combinations. Common mistake is forgetting to filter for completed trips or not using proper partitioning in the window function.
